title: May 2026 GitHub Trends: AI Agents & Code Intelligence
full_text:
Back to digest AI Agents and Autonomous Coding
The biggest story in this week's trending repos is the explosion of AI agent platforms and coding assistants. harry0703/MoneyPrinterTurbo leads the charge with an impressive 4,698 stars today, offering one-click AI video generation capabilities. Close behind, Lum1104/Understand-Anything gained 3,776 stars for its interactive code knowledge graphs that work across multiple AI coding platforms.
anthropics/claude-code (+319 stars) represents Anthropic's entry into terminal-based coding assistance, while openai/codex (+331 stars) offers OpenAI's lightweight coding agent alternative. NousResearch/hermes-agent (+1,411 stars) positions itself as "the agent that grows with you," suggesting adaptive learning capabilities.
Memory and persistence are becoming key differentiators, with rowboatlabs/rowboat (+81 stars) providing an open-source AI coworker with memory, and thedotmack/claude-mem (+298 stars) offering persistent context across agent sessions.
Developer Tooling and Workflow Enhancement
Infrastructure tooling shows strong momentum with earendil-works/pi (+627 stars) providing a comprehensive AI agent toolkit including CLI, TUI, and web interfaces. ryoppippi/ccusage (+250 stars) addresses a practical need by analyzing token usage and costs for coding agents.
Document processing and parsing tools are gaining traction as developers seek better ways to ingest diverse data formats. microsoft/markitdown (+1,410 stars) converts office documents to Markdown, while run-llama/liteparse (+932 stars) offers fast document parsing capabilities. adithya-s-k/omniparse (+100 stars) focuses on optimizing any data format for GenAI frameworks.
Web scraping and data extraction tools are also trending, with firecrawl/firecrawl (+504 stars) providing large-scale web interaction capabilities and unclecode/crawl4ai (+154 stars) offering LLM-friendly web crawling.
Specialized AI Applications
Vertical-specific AI tools are emerging across multiple domains. mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills (+737 stars) provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills for AI agents, mapped to major security frameworks. shiyu-coder/Kronos (+357 stars) introduces a foundation model specifically for financial markets.
OpenMOSS/MOSS-TTS (+71 stars) tackles text-to-speech with support for complex real-world scenarios including multi-speaker dialogue and environmental sound effects.
Enterprise and Business Tools
Business application alternatives continue to gain popularity. twentyhq/twenty (+493 stars) positions itself as an open-source Salesforce alternative designed for AI integration. OpenStock (+247 stars) provides a free alternative to expensive market data platforms.
NangoHQ/nango (+269 stars) focuses on building product integrations with AI assistance, while mastra-ai/mastra (+65 stars) from the Gatsby team offers a framework for AI-powered applications using modern TypeScript.
Security and Compliance
Security tooling reflects growing enterprise adoption of AI systems. microsoft/RAMPART (+62 stars) introduces pytest-native safety testing for agentic AI applications, while p-e-w/heretic (+372 stars) takes the controversial approach of automatic censorship removal for language models.
The trending repositories this week signal that developers are moving beyond basic AI integration toward sophisticated, memory-enabled agents and specialized vertical applications. The focus on tooling, monitoring, and security infrastructure suggests the ecosystem is maturing rapidly as AI-powered development becomes mainstream.

Today's GitHub trending list is dominated by AI agent tooling, from browser-control agents to multi-provider LLM gateways and governance frameworks for autonomous systems.

Today's GitHub trending list is dominated by AI agents, MCP infrastructure, and the tooling builders need to wire them together productively.
